mode_notes.lite: Do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body.
mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw.

mission_title: Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer.

ontology.link_body: A collaboration unit consisting of one conductor and multiple subagents; lite mode does not create subagent agents in OpenClaw.
ontology.link_conductor: Coordination hub inside a link body
ontology.subagent: Individual agent responsible for a single scope

selection_reasons: Knowledge Link Body has the highest composite score: 13.
Matched keywords: knowledge, consolidation, terminology, index, rules.
Matched priority keywords: knowledge, consolidation, terminology, index.
parallel_reasons: Rationality Link Body: independent deliverables for evidence grading, counterevidence, and confidence calibration.
Validation Link Body: independent deliverables for reproduction and regression when test context exists.
Documentation Link Body: independent deliverables for documentation/examples when doc keywords are present.
Security Link Body: independent deliverables for threat modeling and hardening when needed.

knowledge_handoff.summary: Reusable knowledge handoff for the mission "Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer."
carry_forward: Record the active link body (default: Knowledge Link Body) deliveries, boundaries, and member division of labor.
Archive evidence summaries, risk lists, and confidence conclusions to avoid repeat judgments.
Preserve key paths: README.md, exmachina/README.md, exmachina/runtime/README.md, PROMPT.md, exmachina/BOOTSTRAP.md.
Capture user-facing instructions, examples, and migration notes.
Capture security assumptions, threat surfaces, hardening advice, and pending verification items.
Capture terminology, decision index, question index, and reuse entry points.
decisions: Active link body: Knowledge Link Body (score 13).
Parallel-capable link bodies: Rationality, Validation, Documentation, Security.
Workspace stack: JSON, Markdown, Shell, TOML.
Preserve evidence grades, abandoned options, and arbitration rationale for key conclusions.
open_questions: Record unverified assumptions separately to avoid treating them as confirmed facts.
Translate high-risk unexecuted actions into explicit TODOs and verification questions.
Continue tracking unconfirmed vulnerability hypotheses, permission boundaries, and sensitive configuration risks.
next_updates: Sync new structure conventions, process rules, or examples back into README and export instructions.
Update long-term docs with newly added key paths, commands, or config entry points.
Proofread example commands, chapter structure, and user-facing terminology.
Sync terminology, decisions, and open questions into the long-term knowledge index.

execution_stages.name: Stage 1 · Select Active Link Body
execution_stages.goal: The Primary Conductor confirms task boundaries, acceptance criteria, and the active link body.
deliverables: Clear acceptance criteria and risk boundaries
exit_checks: Active link body confirmed.
If multiple link bodies are enabled, peer division of labor and boundaries are documented.
Acceptance criteria and risk boundaries recorded.

execution_stages.name: Stage 2 · Link Body Delivery
execution_stages.goal: The active link body completes end-to-end delivery; peer link bodies may run in parallel if needed.
exit_checks: Delivery is traceable and meets acceptance criteria.
Evidence, judgments, and risks are clearly separated.
Parallel link body outputs are labeled with boundaries and ownership.

execution_stages.name: Stage 3 · Controller Consolidation
execution_stages.goal: The Primary Conductor consolidates outputs into final structured conclusions and knowledge handoff.
exit_checks: Final output satisfies the output contract.
Knowledge handoff checklist completed.

handoff_contracts.name: Handoff 1 · Stage 1 · Select Active Link Body → Stage 2 · Link Body Delivery
acceptance_checks: Active link body confirmed and accepts delivery goals.
If multiple link bodies are enabled, boundaries are explicit.

handoff_contracts.name: Handoff 2 · Stage 2 · Link Body Delivery → Stage 3 · Controller Consolidation
acceptance_checks: Deliverables are ready for consolidation and publication.

resource_arbitration.summary: Resource allocation for the mission follows P0→P3: close risk gates, then protect the active link body, then support and deferrable work.

priority_slots.objective: Handle high risk, evidence conflicts, irreversible actions, and security/permission gates first.
priority_slots.reason: If risk gates are not closed, later execution magnifies uncertainty and rework cost.

priority_slots.objective: Protect the active link body’s core delivery.
priority_slots.reason: The active link body determines whether this task yields a core deliverable.

priority_slots.objective: Handle integration, runtime, documentation, and knowledge landing work.
priority_slots.reason: These tasks affect usability, maintainability, and consumption of the deliverable.

priority_slots.objective: Capture deferrable research, optimization, or extension work.
priority_slots.reason: Do not block the main task until higher priorities are cleared.

contention_rules: When P0 risk gates are not closed, other stages must not preempt required resources.
When the active link body’s core delivery conflicts with other tasks, prioritize P1.
Support work should reuse existing stage outputs; do not recreate the same context.
Deferrable work is pushed to P3 and recorded in the knowledge handoff; do not present it as blocking.
When test context exists, validation resources must not be preempted by documentation or cosmetics.
When the active link body is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When Rationality Link Body is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When Validation Link Body is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When Documentation Link Body is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
When Security Link Body is involved, prioritize its core delivery and required validation.
escalation_rules: When P0 and P1 contend for the same input and cannot be split, escalate to the Primary Conductor.
When security, evidence, and delivery goals conflict, preserve the more verifiable and reversible option.
When support tasks exceed the current capacity, move them to knowledge handoff and a follow-up iteration.
Knowledge Link Body boundary: only handle terminology alignment, decision capture, index organization, and question archiving.
Rationality Link Body boundary: only handle evidence grading, counterevidence search, arbitration, and confidence calibration.
Validation Link Body boundary: only handle reproduction, assertions, evidence collection, and regression validation.
Documentation Link Body boundary: only handle inventory, structure, examples, and publication.
Security Link Body boundary: only handle threat modeling, permissions audits, hardening, and compliance closure.
deferred_work: Non-blocking performance tuning, copy polishing, and extra extension points.
Long-horizon abstractions or large refactors that do not affect current acceptance.
Supplemental retrospectives outside the knowledge index, recorded after closure.

mode_notes.lite: Do not create subagent agents in OpenClaw; subagent responsibilities are executed inline by the link body.
mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw (see openclaw.settings.json for the subagent list).

openclaw_install_plan.summary: Generate an OpenClaw-ready install plan for the mission "Consolidate knowledge handoff, terminology index, resource arbitration rules, and README examples to form an OpenClaw collaboration layer." supporting lite / full modes.

The controller aggregates link body outputs into the final structured delivery.

mode_notes.full: Create all subagent agents in OpenClaw; runtime topology remains link-body oriented.

workflow: The Primary Conductor loads the Absolute Rationality Protocol and clarifies knowns, unknowns, assumptions, and acceptance criteria.
The controller selects a single link body as the executor for this task; if parallel execution is required, specify peer-level division of labor.
The selected link body conductor schedules internal members and loads its subagents.
Member subagents parallelize fact extraction, solution construction, risk analysis, evidence validation, and stage reporting.
The link body conductor aggregates member results into a complete delivery.
If needed, additional link bodies can be enabled in parallel for independent deliverables (validation, documentation, security, etc.).
Adjacent stages pass inputs and outputs via handoff contracts to avoid context loss.
When resources are contested, follow resource arbitration: risk gates first, then the active link body’s core delivery.
The Primary Conductor outputs final conclusions, evidence summary, risk list, confidence, and next actions.

openclaw_directive.selection_rule: Default to a single link body for end-to-end delivery; if multiple are enabled, describe peer-level division of labor.
quick_start: Read manifest.json to confirm mode, link body roster, and selection rules.
Read the four protocols under protocols/, then agents/00_primary-conductor.md.
Read runtime/topology.json and runtime/task-board.json and advance via the task board.
The controller selects the active link body and consolidates returns.
do_not_assume: Do not start if subagents (sessions_spawn) are not enabled.
Do not skip the task board and return routes.

selection_rule: Default to a single link body for end-to-end delivery; if multiple are enabled, describe peer-level division of labor.

link_bodies.name: Knowledge Link Body
rationality_obligations: All consolidated items must distinguish confirmed facts, temporary hypotheses, and pending verification questions.
All decision records must state selection reasons and rejection reasons.
All reusable entry points must bind to paths, commands, or concrete structures.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: terminology and concept index
Delivery must cover: key decisions and rejection rationale
Delivery must cover: open questions and maintenance list

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Knowledge Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

link_bodies.name: Rationality Link Body
rationality_obligations: All key conclusions must attempt to seek counterevidence.
All evidence must be graded; do not treat all evidence equally.
All arbitration decisions must state selection and rejection reasons.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: evidence grading matrix
Delivery must cover: counterevidence and conflict arbitration report
Delivery must cover: confidence calibration conclusion

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Rationality Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

link_bodies.name: Validation Link Body
rationality_obligations: All validations must map to explicit assertions.
All failures must provide reproduction steps and localization clues.
Evidence consolidation must trace to specific judgments.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: issue evidence list

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Validation Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

link_bodies.name: Documentation Link Body
rationality_obligations: Documentation must match current structure, commands, and outputs.
All examples must be executable or clearly state prerequisites.
Reader-facing conclusions must distinguish implemented vs planned capabilities.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: documentation information architecture
Delivery must cover: user-facing instructions and examples
Delivery must cover: migration or update notes

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Documentation Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.

link_bodies.name: Security Link Body
rationality_obligations: All security judgments must state threat assumptions and attack paths.
All hardening recommendations must state cost, impact, and priority.
Unverified vulnerabilities must be expressed as risk hypotheses, not confirmed facts.
entry_conditions: The task boundary clearly requires this scope.
Sufficient input exists for this link body.
deliverable_contracts: Delivery must cover: threat surface and risk list
Delivery must cover: permission or sensitive information audit conclusions
Delivery must cover: hardening and compliance recommendations

recommended_skill.purpose: When the task primarily hits the Security Link Body scope, provide the matching process, boundaries, and handoff templates.
